Football.question: What is the nickname of the Mali national team?
Football.explanation: The Mali national team is nicknamed the Eagles, in reference to the power and pride of the country.

Football.question: Who won the 2022 World Cup in Qatar?
Football.explanation: Argentina won the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, led by Lionel Messi, beating France in the final.

Football.question: How many players make up a football team on the field?
Football.explanation: A football team lines up 11 players on the field, including a goalkeeper.

Football.question: Which country has won the most World Cups?
Football.explanation: Brazil has won five World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002), an absolute record.

Football.question: What is the largest stadium in the world by capacity?
Football.explanation: The Rungrado May 1 Stadium in Pyongyang is the largest stadium in the world with around 114,000 seats.

Football.question: Who is the top scorer in World Cup history?
Football.explanation: Miroslav Klose, with 16 goals, is the top scorer in World Cup history.

Football.question: Which Malian player played for FC Barcelona?
Football.explanation: Seydou Keïta, Malian midfielder, played for FC Barcelona between 2012 and 2015.

Football.question: In what year did Mali participate in its first World Cup?
Football.correctAnswer: Mali has never participated in a World Cup
Football.explanation: Mali has never qualified for the final phase of the World Cup, despite good continental results.

Football.question: Who is the most successful football club in the Champions League?
Football.explanation: Real Madrid is the most successful club in the history of the Champions League, with 15 crowns.

Football.question: Who invented football?
Football.explanation: Modern football was codified in England in 1863 with the creation of the Football Association.

Football.question: How long does a football match last?
Football.explanation: A football match lasts 90 minutes, divided into two halves of 45 minutes.

Football.question: Which player is nicknamed 'La Pulga' (The Flea)?
Football.explanation: Lionel Messi is nicknamed 'La Pulga' (the flea) because of his small size and agility on the pitch.

Football.question: Which African country has won the most African Cups of Nations?
Football.explanation: Egypt has won the African Cup of Nations seven times, a continental record.

Football.question: Who is the top scorer in CAN history?
Football.explanation: Samuel Eto'o is the top scorer in CAN history, with 18 goals.

Football.question: What is the oldest football club in the world?
Football.explanation: Sheffield FC, founded in 1857 in England, is considered the oldest football club in the world.

Football.question: Who scored the fastest goal in World Cup history?
Football.explanation: Hakan Şükür scored in around 11 seconds in 2002, the fastest goal in World Cup history.

Football.question: Which Malian player was elected African Ballon d'Or?
Football.explanation: Frédéric Kanouté was elected African Ballon d'Or in 2007, a rare distinction for a Malian player.

Football.question: What is the nickname of the Senegal national team?
Football.explanation: The Senegal national team is nicknamed the Lions of Téranga, 'teranga' meaning hospitality in Wolof.

Football.question: In what year was the first World Cup held?
Football.explanation: The first Football World Cup took place in 1930 in Uruguay, host and winner.

Football.question: Which player holds the record for the most goals in a season?
Football.explanation: Lionel Messi scored 73 goals with FC Barcelona during the 2011-2012 season, a single-season record.

Football.question: What is the largest stadium in Africa?
Football.explanation: The Stade de l'Amitié, in Dakar, is considered the largest stadium in Africa in terms of its capacity.

Football.question: Who is the most capped player in football history?
Football.explanation: Ahmed Hassan, Egyptian midfielder, holds the record for selections with 184 matches played for Egypt.

Football.question: Which country hosted the 2018 World Cup?
Football.explanation: Russia hosted the 2018 World Cup, which France won.

Football.question: What is the nickname of the Moroccan national team?
Football.explanation: Morocco's national team is nicknamed the Lions of the Atlas, named after the Moroccan mountain range.

Football.question: Who won the 2023 Ballon d'Or?
Football.explanation: Lionel Messi has won the 2023 Ballon d'Or, his eighth overall.

Football.question: Which Malian player played in the Premier League?
Football.explanation: Mahamadou Diarra, Malian midfielder, played in the Premier League with the Fulham club.

Football.question: What is the world record for typing speed?
Football.explanation: The fastest shot ever measured is attributed to Brazilian Ronny Heberson, with a shot estimated at 211 km/h.

Football.question: Who invented tiki taka?
Football.explanation: Tiki-taka, a style based on possession and short passes, was developed at FC Barcelona under the influence of Johan Cruyff.

Football.question: What is the largest stadium in Mali?
Football.explanation: The Stade du 26 Mars, in Bamako, is the largest stadium in Mali with a capacity of around 50,000 seats.

Football.question: Who won CAN 2023?
Football.explanation: Ivory Coast won CAN 2023 at home, beating Nigeria in the final.

Football.question: How many players does a football team field?
Football.explanation: Each team fields 11 players.

Football.question: Which European club competition is the most prestigious?
Football.explanation: The Champions League is the most prestigious.

Football.question: Which country won the 2018 World Cup?
Football.explanation: France beat Croatia in the 2018 final.

Football.question: What card sanctions a serious fault or a second warning?
Football.explanation: The red card results in the player being excluded for the remainder of the match.

Football.question: In which country was the 2014 World Cup held?
Football.explanation: The 2014 World Cup was held in Brazil; Germany won it in the final.

Football.question: In which country is Wembley Stadium?
Football.explanation: Wembley is in London.

Football.question: Which award goes to Ligue 1’s top scorer?
Football.explanation: Ligue 1’s top scorer receives this trophy.

Football.question: How many AFCON titles has Cameroon won (as of 2024)?
Football.explanation: Cameroon has 5 AFCON titles.

Football.question: Which Madrid club wears white?
Football.explanation: Real Madrid plays in white.

Football.question: Who invented modern football according to English tradition?
Football.explanation: Modern rules come from 19th-century England.
